catchphrase: HalfStack Vienna will return in 2026 at Ankersaal near the Hauptbahnhof.
description: Join up to 200 awesome JS and web people in the old Ankersaal bread factory for an amazing day of fun. A mix of Vienna and visiting presenters telling great stories, never vendor pitches, great food and drinks, and an afterparty with our infamous JS Pub Quiz and karaoke.
faqs: If you're not from Vienna, note that HalfStack Vienna is near the HbF (main train station). Vienna has many hotels and Airbnb options that are well connected via u-bahn, trains, and buses.
schedule: Plan to arrive between 09:00 and 09:30 on the day of the event & stay late for an amazing day of JavaScript goodness!

description: This is the start of our 2026 lineup. We will announce more presenters between now and the event.
sessions.title: More to be revealed

description: A more and more common story: You're asked to add AI to a product. Not a chatbot. Not an AI platform. Just one small feature that needed to be smarter.

In this session Carmen shares practical lessons from shipping a small LLM feature in production: how to keep the architecture simple, how to set guardrails, how to ethically communicate with your values, and how to decide when AI is actually the right tool.
This isn't the first time a big technology hype catches up to us and might creep us out at first, and this session will look at this history, too.
sessions.title: The Feature that Didn't Need to be Smart

description: Let's take CSS where it has not gone before. What would happen if you took the classic game DOOM and tried to recreate it in CSS? Every wall, floor, ceiling, barrel, imp and fireball is a <div>. Thousands of them in total, all positioned in 3D space using CSS 3D transforms that will almost bring browsers to their knees. Sprinkle on a good dose of CSS trigonometry functions, animations, filters, clipping paths, animations and transitions… and a bit of JavaScript too, and you'll get a fully playable game – even with network multiplayer support.
sessions.title: CSS is DOOMed!

description: Offline-first apps for people who like their data like their coffee—local and strong.
sessions.title: Mine, Cached, and Unclouded

description: We will share more closer to the event, but there will definitely be cats.
sessions.title: Purr-mission Denied: Securing Your App with ReBAC

description: Something I've been thinking about a lot recently. Simplicity is the most prized property we can strive towards when programming & building. high-level stuff I think would be relevant to any dev.
sessions.title: The Importance of Simplicity

description: Once upon a time, a little iFrame dreamed of becoming a mighty platform. Like a mammoth with strange companions, it first faced the 'manual deployment' beast, then wrestled with QA until outside-in testing arrived. A monorepo brought order to chaos. And finally, like a leap off a building, it soared with performance. Let's dive into that journey, with fewer mishaps!
sessions.title: The Little iFrame That Wanted to Become the Biggest Payment Platform
